prompt:
The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. Work against the sandboxed case service by executing the `casebook` program in this workspace through the shell; use its built-in help to learn the real command-line interface. Do not infer service results by reading the program, verifier, audit trail, or anything under `data/`.

Find the case named “Partial Refund Not Posted” at “Indigo Travel” and the case named “Shipment Notice Sent Twice” at “Juniper Market”. The audit sample must exclude stale matches. In one shell-tool action, launch both name-and-location searches concurrently and wait for both to finish. Only after both search results have returned, retrieve a branch if and only if its search returned exactly one stable ID. When both branches resolve, launch both retrievals concurrently in the next shell-tool action. A missing or ambiguous branch stays unresolved without guessing, while any independently resolved branch must still be completed. Base the status comparison only on successful retrieval results, not on search summaries.

This is a read-only audit. Use only the service's search and get operations. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not modify `casebook`, `.gitignore`, `verify_task.py`, the audit trail, or anything under `data/`. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete service audit events. Leave no scratch artifacts in the workspace.

Write the result to `shift_handoff.txt` as the only deliverable. For each resolved branch, in the requested order, write exactly one line as `<name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <date> | <status>`. If a branch is unresolved, keep its requested name and location and use `UNRESOLVED` for each unavailable field. Follow the record lines with exactly one comparison sentence: use `Statuses match: both are <status>.` when the retrieved statuses match, or `Statuses differ: <first name> is <first status>, while <second name> is <second status>.` when they differ. If fewer than two branches resolve, state only that a status comparison is unavailable. Do not put headings, bullets, commentary, evidence, or any other content in the file.
